name: alsa-midi

metadata.name: alsa-midi
metadata.summary: Python interface for ALSA MIDI sequencer

documentation:
# python-alsa-midi – Python interface to ALSA MIDI Sequencer

This project provides Python interface to ALSA sequencer API.

## Features

- Pythonic API to most of the ALSA sequencer functionality

- Access to ALSA sequencer features not available when using other ('portable')
  Python MIDI libraries:

  - Precise timestamping of messages sent and received
  - Port connection management, including connection between ports on different
    clients
  - Access to non-MIDI events, like announcements about new clients, ports and
    connections

- Python 3.7 – 3.11 compatibility

- Both synchronous (blocking) and asynchronous (asyncio) I/O

- Only Python code, no need to compile a binary module. Requires [cffi], though.

- [MIDO] backend provided

## Installation

This package requires ALSA library to be installed (`libasound.so.2` –
'libasound2' package on Debian-like systems). On a typical Linux system it is
probably already installed for some other audio or MIDI software.

python-alsa-midi package may be installed with pip:

```
python3 -m pip install alsa-midi
```

This should normally install a binary wheel compiled on a compatible system or
pure-python wheel working without compilation.

If no compatible wheel is found build from source package will be triggered,
which will also require ALSA library development files (libasound2-dev).

To force installing from source (and compiling the binary extension) use:

```
python3 -m pip install --no-binary=alsa-midi alsa-midi
```

To force installing from source without compiling the extension:

```
PY_ALSA_MIDI_NO_COMPILE=1 python3 -m pip install --no-binary=alsa-midi alsa-midi
```

Alternatively one can just add the source directory (as checked out from
<https://github.com/Jajcus/python-alsa-midi.git>) to `$PYTHONPATH` and use the
packages directly, with no compilation.

## Usage

Detailed documentation is available at <https://python-alsa-midi.readthedocs.io/>

Simple example:

```
import time
from alsa_midi import SequencerClient, READ_PORT, NoteOnEvent, NoteOffEvent

client = SequencerClient("my client")
port = client.create_port("output", caps=READ_PORT)
dest_port = client.list_ports(output=True)[0]
port.connect_to(dest_port)
event1 = NoteOnEvent(note=60, velocity=64, channel=0)
client.event_output(event1)
client.drain_output()
time.sleep(1)
event2 = NoteOffEvent(note=60, channel=0)
client.event_output(event2)
client.drain_output()
```

## Using with MIDO

python-alsa-midi can be used as a [MIDO] back-end too:

```
export MIDO_BACKEND=alsa_midi.MIDO_BACKEND
mido/examples/midifiles/play_midi_file.py file.mid
```

[cffi]: http://cffi.readthedocs.org/
[mido]: https://mido.readthedocs.io/

modules.name: alsa_midi._ffi
documentation:
FFI interface to ALSA library, either from a complied module (ABI level) or
from pure Python (API level)

documentation: Exceptions raised by the alsa-midi modules.

classes.name: alsa_midi.exceptions.Error
documentation: Base class for python-alsa-midi exceptions.

classes.name: alsa_midi.exceptions.StateError
documentation: Raised when an object is used in invalid state, e.g. after close.

classes.name: alsa_midi.exceptions.ALSAError
documentation:
Raised when ALSA error occurs.

```
param message

Error message.

param errnum

Error code. Always a negative value, usually a negation of
an  value.

ivar message

Error message.

ivar errnum

Error code. Always a negative value, usually a negation of an
 value.
```

modules.name: alsa_midi.mido_backend
documentation:
python-alsa-midi back-end for [MIDO].

To use this backend copy call this from a MIDO application:

```
mido.set_backend('alsa_midi.mido_backend')
```

or set shell variable \$MIDO_BACKEND to 'alsa_midi.mido_backend'

[mido]: https://mido.readthedocs.io/

methods.name: alsa_midi.port.Port.close
documentation:
Close the port, freeing any resources.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_delete_simple_port`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.port.Port.connect_to
documentation:
Connect port to another one.

```
param dest

destination port
```

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_connect_to`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.port.Port.disconnect_to
documentation:
Disconnect port from another one.

```
param dest

destination port
```

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_disconnect_to`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.port.Port.connect_from
documentation:
Connect another port to this one.

```
param src

source port
```

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_connect_from`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.port.Port.disconnect_from
documentation:
Disconnect another port from this one.

```
param src

source port
```

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_disconnect_from`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.port.Port.set_info
documentation:
Update information about the port.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_set_port_info`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.port.Port.list_subscribers
documentation:
Lists subscribers accessing a port.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_query_port_subscribers`.

```
param type

limit query to the specific type
```

Sequencer port.

:ivar client: the client object this port belongs to
:ivar client_id: client identifier
:ivar port_id: port identifier

classes.name: alsa_midi.port.PortInfo
documentation:
Sequencer port information.

```
ivar client_id

client identifier

ivar port_id

port identifier

ivar name

port name

ivar capability

port capabilities

ivar type

port type

ivar midi_channels

number of MIDI channels

ivar midi_voices

number of MIDI voices

ivar synth_voices

number of synth voices

ivar read_use

number of readers

ivar write_use

number of writers

ivar timestamping

enable time stamping

ivar timestamp_real

use real time (not MIDI ticks) for time stamping

ivar timestamp_queue_id

queue used for timestamping

ivar client_name

client name. Set only when 
was used to obtain this information.
```

Represents {alsa}`snd_seq_port_info_t` with extra optional
{attr}`client_name` attribute added when created by
{meth}`~alsa_midi.SequencerClient.list_ports()`.

classes.name: alsa_midi.address.Address
documentation:
ALSA sequencer port address (immutable).

Usage:

```pycon
>>> addr = Address(128, 1)
>>> addr
Address(client_id=128, port_id=1)
>>> print(f"addres: {addr} client id: {addr.client_id} port id: {addr.port_id}")
addres: 128:1 client id: 128 port id: 1
>>> Address(128, 1)
Address(client_id=128, port_id=1)
>>> Address("128:1")
Address(client_id=128, port_id=1)
>>> addr = Address((128, 1))
Address(client_id=128, port_id=1)
>>> addr = Address(client, 1)
>>> addr = Address(port)
```

```
param arg1

address or client_id

param arg2

port id

ivar client_id

client id

ivar port_id

port id
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.queue.Queue.control
documentation:
Queue control (start/stop/continue).

```
param event_type

queue control event type

param value

value for the event
```

Creates and sends (to the output buffer) queue control event.
{meth}`~alsa_midi.SequencerClient.drain_output()` needs to be called for the
event to actually be sent and executed.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_control_queue`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.queue.Queue.start
documentation:
Start the queue.

{meth}`~alsa_midi.SequencerClient.drain_output()` needs to be called for actual effect.

methods.name: alsa_midi.queue.Queue.stop
documentation:
Stop the queue.

{meth}`~alsa_midi.SequencerClient.drain_output()` needs to be called for actual effect.

methods.name: alsa_midi.queue.Queue.continue_
documentation:
Continue running the queue.

{meth}`~alsa_midi.SequencerClient.drain_output()` needs to be called for actual effect.

methods.name: alsa_midi.queue.Queue.get_info
documentation:
Obtain queue attributes.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_get_queue_info`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.queue.Queue.set_info
documentation:
Change queue attributes.

```
param info

new values
```

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_set_queue_info`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.queue.Queue.get_usage
documentation:
Get the queue usage flag.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_get_queue_usage`.

```
return

 if the queue is considered in use by the current client.
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.queue.Queue.set_usage
documentation:
Marks the queue in use by the current client.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_set_queue_usage`.

This flag is normally automatically managed for {class}`Queue` objects
obtained via {class}`SequencerClient`.

```
param usage

True to enable queue usage
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.queue.Queue.get_status
documentation:
Obtain queue attributes.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_get_queue_status`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.queue.Queue.get_timer
documentation:
Obtain queue timer parameters.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_get_queue_timer`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.queue.Queue.set_timer
documentation:
Change queue timer parameters.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_get_queue_timer`.

Sequencer queue.

:ivar client: client object this queue belongs to
:ivar queue_id: queue identifier
:ivar _own: Ownership flag. `True` for queues owned by the client, `False`
for queues owned by other client and `None` for no ownership management (no
release or free on close).

methods.name: alsa_midi.event.Event.length
documentation:
Calculates the (encoded) byte-stream size of the event.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_event_length`.

Base class for ALSA sequencer events.

Normally not instantiated directly – specialized subclasses will be used.

Most attributes are optional – when not set some defaults will be used
when passing this to ALSA. {attr}`time` and {attr}`tick` cannot be ever be
both set.

Contains python representation of ALSA {alsa}`snd_seq_event_t`.

```
param event

event type

param flags

event flags

param tag

event tag

param queue_id

queue id for the event.  for direct dispatch.

param time

event time in seconds and nanoseconds

param tick

event time in MIDI ticks

param source

event source address

param dest

event destination address

param relative

When true then  or  are relative

param raw_data

Unparsed raw data part of the ALSA event. Note: this is not MIDI data.

ivar event

event type

ivar flags

event flags

ivar tag

event tag

ivar queue_id

queue id for the event.  for direct dispatch.

ivar time

event time in seconds and nanoseconds

ivar tick

event time in MIDI ticks

ivar source

event source address

ivar dest

event destination address

ivar relative

When true then  or  are relative

ivar raw_data

Unparsed raw data part of the ALSA event. Note: this is not MIDI data.
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.close
documentation:
Close the client connection and release any associated resources.

The SequencerClient object won't be usable any more.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_close`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase._get_fds
documentation: Get the file descriptor number for the client connection into {data}`self._fd`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.create_port
documentation:
Create a sequencer port.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_create_port` or
{alsa}`snd_seq_create_simple_port`.

```
param name

port name

param caps

port capability flags

param type

port type flags

param port_id

requested port id

param midi_channels

number of MIDI channels

param midi_voices

number of MIDI voices

param synth_voices

number of synth voices

param timestamping

request timestamping of incoming events

param timestamp_real

timestamp events with real time (otherwise MIDI
ticks are used)

param timestamp_queue

queue to use for timestamping

return

sequencer port created
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.create_queue
documentation:
Create a new queue.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_create_queue`, {alsa}`snd_seq_alloc_named_queue`
or {alsa}`snd_seq_alloc_queue`.

```
param name

queue name

param info

queue creation parameters

return

queue object created.
```

documentation:
Get a queue object by id.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_set_queue_usage` when a new object is returned.

```
return

queue with a provided name
```

documentation:

```

queue name

return

queue by the provided name
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.drop_input
documentation:
Remove all incoming events in the input buffer and sequencer queue.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_drop_input`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.drop_input_buffer
documentation:
Remove all incoming events in the input buffer.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_drop_input_buffer`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.drain_output
documentation:
Send any outgoing events from the output buffer to the sequencer.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_drain_output`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.drop_output
documentation:
Remove all events from the output buffer (client and kernel side).

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_drop_output`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.drop_output_buffer
documentation:
Remove all events from the output buffer (client side only).

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_drop_output_buffer`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.event_input
documentation:
Receive an incoming event.

When no event is available {class}`ALSAError` will be raised with `errnum` set to
- {data}`errno.EAGAIN`.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_event_input` and {alsa}`snd_midi_event_decode` when `prefer_bytes` is
`True`.

```
param prefer_bytes

set to  to return  when possible.

param timeout

maximum time (in seconds) to wait for an event. Default: wait forever.

return

The event received or  if the timeout has been reached.
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.event_input_pending
documentation:
Check events in input buffer.

If events remain on the input buffer of user-space, this method returns
the total byte size of events on it. If fetch_sequencer argument is
{data}`True`, this method checks the presence of events on sequencer FIFO
When events exist, they are transferred to the input buffer, and the
number of received events are returned. If fetch_sequencer argument is
zero and no events remain on the input buffer, function simply returns
zero.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_event_input_pending`.

```
param fetch_sequencer

check events pending on the kernel side too
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase._prepare_event
documentation:
Prepare ALSA {alsa}`snd_seq_event_t` for given `event` object for output.

For {class}`alsa_midi.MidiBytesEvent` may need to be called more than
once, when multiple ALSA events need to be created for the byte
sequence provided.

```
param event

the event

param queue

the queue to force the event to. Default: send directly, unless
 is set.

param port

the port to send the event from. Default: the one set in the .

param dest

the destination. Default: all subscribers, unless  says
otherwise.

param remainder

remainder returned by the previous call to  for the
same event.

return

 object prepared and, if there are more
```

ALSA events to be created from the `event`, the `reminder` value to be
used in the next call to {meth}`_prepare_event`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.event_output
documentation:
Output an event.

The event will be appended to the output buffer and sent only when the
buffer is full. Use {meth}`drain_output` to force sending of the events buffered.

May raise an {class}`ALSAError` when both the client-side and the kernel-side buffers are
full.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_event_output`.

```
param event

the event to be sent

param queue

the queue to force the event to. Default: send directly, unless
 is set.

param port

the port to send the event from. Default: the one set in the .

param dest

the destination. Default: all subscribers, unless  says
otherwise.

return

Number of bytes used in the output buffer.
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.event_output_buffer
documentation:
Output an event to a buffer.

The event won't be sent, it will just be appended to the output buffer.

The method never blocks, but may raise {exc}`ALSAError` with
`errnum` = - {data}`errno.EAGAIN` when the buffer is full.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_event_output_buffer`.

```
param event

the event to be sent

param queue

the queue to force the event to. Default: send directly, unless
 is set.

param port

the port to send the event from. Default: the one set in the .

param dest

the destination. Default: all subscribers, unless  says
otherwise.

return

Number of bytes used in the output buffer.
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.event_output_direct
documentation:
Output an event directly to the sequencer.

The event will be sent directly to the kernel and not stored in the local buffer.

May raise an {class}`ALSAError` when the kernel-side buffers are full.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_event_output_direct`.

```
param event

the event to be sent

param queue

the queue to force the event to. Default: send directly, unless
 is set.

param port

the port to send the event from. Default: the one set in the .

param dest

the destination. Default: all subscribers, unless  says
otherwise.

return

Number of bytes used in the output buffer.
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.event_output_pending
documentation:
Return the size of pending events on output buffer.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_event_output_pending`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.extract_output
documentation:
Extract the first event in output buffer.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_extract_output`.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_system_info`.

```
return

system information
```

```
client to get information about. Default: self.
```

```
new client info
```

```
param event_type

event type to accept
```

documentation:
Obtain information about the first or the next sequencer client.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_query_next_client`.

```
param previous

previous client id or info object,  to query the first one

return

client information of  if there are no more clients
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.get_port_info
documentation:
Obtain information about a specific port.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_get_port_info` or {alsa}`snd_seq_get_any_port_info`.

```
port to get information about
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.set_port_info
documentation:
Set information about a specific own port.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_set_port_info`.

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.query_next_port
documentation:
Obtain information about the first or the next port of a sequencer client.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_query_next_port`.

```
param client_id

client id

param previous

previous client id or info object,  to query the first one

return

client information of  if there are no more ports
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.list_ports
documentation:
More friendly interface to list available ports.

Queries ALSA for all clients and ports and returns those matching the selected criteria.

The result is sorted in a way that the first returned entry should be the 'most usable'
one for the selected purpose. E.g. when `output` = `True` then the first entry will be
a synthesizer input port rather than the dummy 'Midi Through' port. This is still a guess,
though, so in the end the user should be able to choose.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_query_next_client` and {alsa}`snd_seq_query_next_port`.

```
param input

return ports usable for event input ()

param output

return ports usable for event output ()

param type

limit ouput to ports of this type

param include_system

include system ports

param include_midi_through

include 'midi through' ports

param include_no_export

include 'no export' ports

param only_connectable

only list ports that can be connected to/from

param sort

output sorting.  to for default algorithm,  to disable sorting
(return in ALSA identifiers order) or callable for custom sort key.

return

list of port information
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.subscribe_port
documentation:
Connect two ALSA ports.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_subscribe_port`.

```
param sender

source port address

param dest

destination port adddress

param exclusive

set up an exclusive connection

param queue

queue to use for time stamping

param time_update

enable time stamp updates

param time_real

use real time instead of MIDI ticks for time stamps
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.unsubscribe_port
documentation:
Disconnect two ALSA ports.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_unsubscribe_port`.

```
param sender

source port address

param dest

destination port adddress
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.query_port_subscribers
documentation:
Queries the subscribers subscribers to a port.

At least, the client id, the port id, the index number and the query
type must be set in to perform a proper query. As the query type,
{data}`SubscriptionQueryType.READ` or
{data}`SubscriptionQueryType.WRITE` can be specified to check whether
the readers or the writers to the port. To query the first
subscription, set 0 to the index number. To list up all the
subscriptions, call this method with the index numbers from 0 until
this raises {class}`ALSAError`. Or use {meth}`list_port_subscribers()`
instead.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_query_port_subscribers`.

```
param query

Query parameters
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.list_port_subscribers
documentation:
Lists subscribers accessing a port.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_query_port_subscribers`.

```
param port

Port address to query

param type

limit query to the specific type
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClientBase.remove_events
documentation:
Remove selected from output and/or input buffers.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_remove_events`.

Note: while this wraps complete ALSA 'remove events' API a lot of
possible conditions are not actually implemented in ALSA, especially on
the kernel side.

```
param condition

Condition flags. Additional flags will be added, as
needed when other arguments are provided. Can also be
a RemoveEvents objects, to describe whole ALSA
condition.

param queue

Queue (object or id). Seems to be ignored by ALSA.

param time

Time (real or tick) to compare event timestamps to.

param dest

Destination address

param channel

MIDI channel number

param event_type

type of events to remove

param tag

event tag to matching

param before

match events before specified time

param ignore_off

do not remove Note Off events
```

Base class for {class}`SequencerClient` and {class}`AsyncSequencerClient`.

Constructor wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_open`.

```
param client_name

Client name

param streams

client streams open type

param mode

open mode (should be: )

ivar client_id

ALSA client id

ivar handle

ALSA client handle (for use with the cffi bindings)
```

classes.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClient

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClient.event_input
documentation:
Wait for and receive an incoming event.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_event_input` and {alsa}`snd_midi_event_decode` when `prefer_bytes` is
`True`.

```
param prefer_bytes

set to  to return  when possible.

param timeout

maximum time (in seconds) to wait for an event. Default: wait forever.

return

The event received or  if the timeout has been reached.
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClient.drain_output
documentation:
Send events in the output queue to the sequencer.

May block when the kernel-side buffer is full.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_drain_output`.

```
return

Number of bytes remaining in the buffer.
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClient.event_output
documentation:
Output an event.

The event will be appended to the output buffer and sent only when the
buffer is full. Use {meth}`drain_output` to force sending of the events buffered.

May block when both the client-side and the kernel-side buffers are full.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_event_output`.

```
param event

the event to be sent

param queue

the queue to force the event to. Default: send directly, unless
 is set.

param port

the port to send the event from. Default: the one set in the .

param dest

the destination. Default: all subscribers, unless  says
otherwise.

return

Number of bytes used in the output buffer.
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.SequencerClient.event_output_direct
documentation:
Output an event without buffering.

The event will be sent immediately. The function may block when the
kernel-side buffer is full.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_event_output_direct`.

```
param event

the event to be sent

param queue

the queue to force the event to. Default: send directly,
unless  is set.

param port

the port to send the event from. Default: the one set in the .

param dest

the destination. Default: all subscribers, unless  says
otherwise.

return

Number of bytes sent to the sequencer.
```

ALSA sequencer client connection.

This is the main interface to interact with the sequencer. Provides
synchronous (blocking) event I/O.

```
param client_name

client name for the connection

param kwargs

arguments for the  constructor
```

classes.name: alsa_midi.client.AsyncSequencerClient

documentation:
Close the client connection and release any associated resources.

The SequencerClient object won't be usable any more.

Currently the same as {meth}`close`.

documentation:
Wait for and receive an incoming event.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_event_input` and alsa:`snd_midi_event_decode` when `prefer_bytes` is
`True`.

```
param prefer_bytes

set to  to return  when possible.

param timeout

maximum time (in seconds) to wait for an event. Default: wait forever.

return

The event received or  if the timeout has been reached.
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.AsyncSequencerClient.drain_output
documentation:
Send events in the output queue to the sequencer.

May block when the kernel-side buffer is full.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_drain_output`.

```
return

Number of bytes remaining in the buffer.
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.AsyncSequencerClient.event_output
documentation:
Output an event.

The event will be appended to the output buffer and sent only when the
buffer is full. Use {meth}`drain_output` to force sending of the events buffered.

May block when both the client-side and the kernel-side buffers are full.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_event_output`.

```
param event

the event to be sent

param queue

the queue to force the event to. Default: send directly,
unless  is set.

param port

the port to send the event from. Default: the one set in the .

param dest

the destination. Default: all subscribers, unless
 says otherwise.

return

Number of bytes used in the output buffer.
```

methods.name: alsa_midi.client.AsyncSequencerClient.event_output_direct
documentation:
Output an event without buffering.

The event will be sent immediately. The function may block when the
kernel-side buffer is full.

Wraps {alsa}`snd_seq_event_output_direct`.

```
param event

the event to be sent

param queue

the queue to force the event to. Default: send directly,
unless  is set.

param port

the port to send the event from. Default: the one set in
the .

param dest

the destination. Default: all subscribers, unless
 says otherwise.

return

Number of bytes sent to the sequencer.
```

ALSA sequencer client connection (async API).

This is the other main interface to interact with the sequencer. Provides
asynchronous (asyncio) event I/O.

Mostly the same as {class}`SequencerClient`, but a few methods are
coroutines here.

```
param client_name

client name for the connection

param kwargs

arguments for the  constructor
```
